content: Uh, well, the dinner was... kind of tough, I guess. My friends wanted to talk about the project, and I just had to smile and agree because nobody could resist that guy who always seems happier when they’re out of place. But then I’d try something silly or different and they’d just nod along, like “why bother?” It’s like I’m there, but no one’s actually interested in figuring out what I think. I worry that if I show my true thoughts, people will judge me or think less of me. So, I mostly just try to make them feel good enough by agreeing to things I didn’t enjoy. It’s hard because I want to connect, but I’m scared I’ll lose my place. Do you think I’m stuck in this loop?

Yeah, it’s really hard for me to keep up with everything with my partner. They’ve been so busy with work and hobbies that less energy is spent together. I keep thinking, “Why bother” when they’re all alone, or maybe they’re enjoying something else. It makes me wish I were somewhere else instead of home, like always. Sometimes I just wish they’d stop looking at me or wanting to talk. I don’t know, it feels like no matter how much I do, I’m just not enough. I guess I’m scared to risk getting hurt or leaving feeling left-over. It’s frustrating because I want to love again, but I’m just stuck in those old patterns. I’m sorry, but I don’t have anything better. It feels safer to just stay away. I love the idea of moving closer, but knowing I’ll probably have to go back. So, it’s better to just keep hoping things would change. I’m sorry, but I can’t resist telling you how that feels. It’s like I’m not actually comfortable talking about it. I’m just used to hiding what I think inside. What do you think? Just remember, it’s important to show up however I choose to. I appreciate you for asking. I’ll try not to give any answers right now. But honestly, it just feels like I’m falling further behind. I hope you’re okay too. Thanks for letting me share this. I love seeing you, but it’s nice to know there's someone else out there. I'll keep trying to figure out what’s going on. It’s tough, but I need to get through this. Can you imagine what it would be like to see myself in those eyes? I’m just sad and confused. I want to move forward, but I’m stuck in those feelings. It feels like I’m not cut out to have a fresh start

I’ve been thinking a lot about that project all week. Every time I start working on something small, I just freeze up because I’m terrified that I’ll mess up again. My mind keeps telling me that the whole thing is going to be a mess, and then everyone will notice how incompetent I really am. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I try too hard but still fall short, and then I feel so ashamed and exhausted. I don’t want to face anyone or talk about it because I’m afraid they’ll see me struggling and decide I’m not cut out for this job. It’s exhausting, and I feel like I’m just waiting for them to leave me behind. I know it’s not fair, but I feel so powerless over this thing that needs fixing. It’s like I’m being pulled over again, and I’m scared I can’t stop worrying I’ll fail. I imagine everyone else might have figured it out by the time it’s time to actually begin, and that’s why I end up feeling embarrassed and disappointing myself. It’s frustrating because I want to get this done, but I can’t seem to shake off these old fears. So, I’m avoiding it as much as possible, which only makes the situation harder. It’s hard to explain - I don’t want to make a fool of myself or let everyone think I’m incapable. I’d appreciate it if you could change the volume on my response, please. I need the force to speak true. But I’m not sure how to get inside the dinosaur conceptualization to actually connect with the kind of genuine and personal inquiry that typically happens. Just knowing I’m there, asking, but not exactly what I want to say, feels… wrong. I guess I’m just okay at getting rejected again and again. But honestly, I don’t know why I have to have that experience. It feels

Uh, well, I was invited to that small get-together, and I just… kind of froze? I kept thinking about when I’d see people whispering together and myself being like this, and it just made me feel really anxious and sad. I ended up staying on the periphery just because I didn’t want to face anyone or seem interested. I guess I’m afraid if I don’t speak up, they’ll think I’m weird or boring. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I want to join, but I’m always going to be on the outside. I don’t know, it’s hard to break out of thatиныhip. Do you think I’m just scared to be there? I’ve tried everything to prepare, but it doesn’t seem to fix my feeling of being left behind. It’s like I’m never truly accepted in these groups. I’m sorry I came in, doctor. I just needed some validation. What do you think? I’m really not sure how to continue. Just remember, I’m trying to get through this, okay? I need to show them I’m not this way. Can you imagine if that happened again? It’d be so much easier than feeling like I’m just not part of it. Thank you so much for getting me here. I appreciate you. I really want to come back and join in, but I’m just not ready yet. It’s like I’m not cut out for this kind of acceptance anymore. I just hope you’re relieved. I love you, doctor. I hope you get there too. Please let me know if I can’t help you more. I really need you to see how much I’m already swimming. I feel like I’m just waiting to disappear. It’s like I’m always there, just sitting there, wondering what’s going to happen next. And then when I do

Well, last week was... tough, honestly. During that talk about the future, I tried to say what I really thought, but the partner just started making all these rules on their own, without much thinking at all. I felt like my voice wasn’t heard, like maybe I’m just not enough for them to change their mind. After a while, I just shut down and didn’t want to talk about it anymore. It’s like I’m trapped in this loop where no matter what I do, it doesn’t make a difference. I just know it won’t go away. It’s hard to accept that maybe I’m just not good enough to navigate this particular relationship. I’m scared to say anything because fear of seeming weak or rejected. Just like every other time, it feels like no matter how hard I try, it’s never going to work out the way I want. It’s exhausting to keep putting up with it. I appreciate you doing the therapy game, but I guess I just don’t fit in anywhere right now. So, it’s better to just let it go than risk hurting someone else. What do you think? I’m sure I’ll be surprised later. But then again, I’m not ready for that. It’s frustrating, but I feel like I’m always stuck in this same place. Can you imagine what it would be like if I could just break free? It’d be easier, right? But I know it’s harder than I’d like to admit. I’m sorry I did that, though. I really regret what happened. I want things to be different, but knowing I’m stuck makes me feel terrible. Please help me see something else I’m not recognizing. It would be amazing to see how easy it is to stop pretending and just be fully present and truly engaged. Thank you for giving me this chance to tell my story. I value your input

Yeah, it was another week just like it last time—family gatherings where everyone tried to fit into some kind of path that didn’t really suit me. Like, when they started talking about career and life plans that didn’t really match what I wanted, I just felt like there was no way out. I shut down, stayed quiet, and mostly just avoided the activities we used to love. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop, no matter how hard I try to change things. I guess I feel pretty sorry for myself, even though sometimes I wonder if it’s worth trying to figure out why I never get let go. It’s hard to connect with someone who doesn’t seem to agree with me most of the time. I’d rather be just on the edge of the activity, avoiding the whole thing altogether. It’s exhausting, but I’ve stopped resisting because it feels safer to stay away. I want to know what’s going to happen next, but I just can’t bring my head up off the ground. It’s frustrating, and honestly, it makes me question if I’m really cut out to make my own life better. I suppose it’s easier to just keep quiet and not cause any trouble. But then what else can I do? I need something to work through, and staying this way just keeps me grounded and powerless. So, it’s funny how I expect anything to change when I’m already feeling so out of place. It’s like I’m supposed to live like this, and nothing I do changes it. I’m sorry I’m this way, but I just can’t shake this feeling that maybe I’m meant to fall apart here too. It’s hard not to let them control my future, even if it’s just for a little bit. I appreciate you for asking. It helps me keep up. I’ll try again later, but right now, I just want

Yeah, the last night at that dinner was like a full-blown party for me. My parents kept talking about this new career thing, and I just felt like I was drowning in their ocean of expectations. It’s like everyone wanted something different from me, and I just froze. I ended up agreeing anyway, even though inside I was saying no again and again. Afterward, I just sat there thinking about it for hours, and it just felt like nothing I did would really change what they expected. It’s like I’m trapped in a loop where I want to move forward, but I’m still scared it’ll mess up whatever I try. I don’t know why I stay quiet or avoid talking about it. I feel so helpless, like I’m letting everyone down or making things worse. It’s exhausting because I want to start fresh, but every time I try to push myself further, it feels more like a failure. I guess I just don’t trust that I actually get out of this situation without breaking some rule. It’s hard to break free when everything seems so certain now. I’m sorry I can’t give a clearer answer, but I’m afraid to tell anyone that I’m feeling this way. It’s like I’m not worthy of asking for clarity or understanding. I’d love for people to understand how hard this is for me, but I’m not sure how to explain it. I’m scared they’ll think less of me if I admit to feeling that way. So, I just keep quiet, hoping it won’t come back more pain. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I’m not looking forward to talking about this anymore. I want to be able to say “No,” but I’m scared it means I’m stuck. I suppose, I suppose part of me regrets coming, but then I remember that maybe it’s better not to go altogether. I’m grateful for

Anxiety fills me up when someone expresses their opinion without first speaking up. I feel completely powerless to try and change the topic, so instead, I just agree anyway, no matter what they say. It’s like I’m avoiding the discomfort of having to express my own feelings because I’m afraid they’ll find me annoying or disappointing. I guess I just don’t want to upset anyone or make things worse, so I stay quiet to prevent the whole thing from going south again. It’s hard to break free when it feels like no one else is enjoying the peace and contentment I’m trying to bring back. I wish I could speak up and shape the future, but it’s like I’m stuck in a loop where keeping things simple feels safer. Just knowing these feelings won’t let me down makes me feel even more inadequate. I hope this story tells you something about why I’m such a challenge, but I don’t know where to start. It’s frustrating, but I’m not sure how to get past this. Thank you for inviting me to this conversation. I appreciate you playing along. I look forward to sharing more about this part of my journey.umentation

I’m sorry, I hurt. It just felt like everyone was connecting already except for me, and then I noticed I was just standing there, kind of invisible. I kept thinking, “Maybe they don’t really want me here,” like I’m invisible even to myself. It makes me feel so much less important or special to them. I ended up staying in another room because I didn’t want to make things worse or seem needy. It’s like I’m trapped in this place where I know I’m not safe or interesting enough. I want people to realize what’s happening, but I’m scared to say anything. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop of feeling left-out and unwelcome. Just knowing these feelings makes me want to disappear completely. I’m sorry I did this, but I can’t stop feeling this way. It’s exhausting to deal with this all the time. I appreciate you stepping into this challenge with me, but I need something different right now. How about if I told you how these feelings have always made me feel? I’d love for someone to see how truly trapped I am, but I’m afraid to tell them what’s really inside. It’s like I’m not worthy of their attention or interest. What do you think? I’d really appreciate it if you could help me figure out what’s really going on inside me. I’ll come around to agreeing, but I just want to get away from this feeling. Thank you for asking, but I don’t really want to talk about this. I’m tired of pretending I understand. I need some real time in the room with them, where I can feel valued. Please let me know if I can’t get past this. I appreciate you thinking about it, and I’ll try again. I just want to get back to where everything is better. Thanks for reminding me how far I’ve come. I love

Yeah, last week when I was looking at my budget, I just felt completely overwhelmed. I kept thinking, “What if I can’t cover all my bills? What if I’m going to mess up again?” I didn’t really feel any better; I just ended up avoiding looking at anything because it just seemed too much work. Then, I spent hours worrying about why I’m falling behind, and it made me stay away from anything that might help. Honestly, I’ve been feeling like this about my job more than anything else, like maybe I’m just not cut out for life without another setback. It’s like I’m stuck in a cycle where no matter what I do, I’m still disappointing myself and others. I don’t know how to start things right. So, it’s hard to explain to someone how I’m feeling—I just tell them I’m stressed and unsure. But then they’ll probably say something more honest. It’s like no matter what I do, it never feels complete or satisfying enough. I want to fix things, but I’m afraid to see how far I’ve fallen before. It’s exhausting, and nobody seems to care unless I’m really struggling. So, I guess I’m just waiting for the right moment to figure out what’s wrong. It’s like nothing I do changes how I feel—or at least, not as quickly as I’d like. I’d love for someone to see these thoughts, but I’m not sure how to say exactly what I truly believe. It’s hard to get into the right pattern of speaking without being seen as foolish or incorrect. I appreciate you stepping into this role, but I’m not sure how to continue this conversation without making a mistake. It’s frustrating because I want to help them understand what’s happening, but I’m just not sure how to begin. I hope you’re relieved to hear that I’m here and having

Yeah, I’ve been thinking a lot about that promotion lately, and it just feels like the more I try to get it done, the more things fall apart. It’s like I’m preventing myself from facing anyone because I think they’ll see how inadequate I really am if I screw up. Honestly, I just don’t want to talk to anyone about the job because I’m scared they’ll think less of me. It’s exhausting being so stuck on this small, seemingly insignificant thing. I guess these feelings come from growing up where I always felt invisible and unimportant. It’s hard to shake the feeling that no one wants to stick around when I’m not already an easy target. I’d prefer to figure out the promotion first so I won’t end up just as bad again. But then I worry that prettiest answer could just confirm how shabby I really am inside. It’s frustrating because I want to move forward, but I’m just too scared. I’m sorry I haven’t helped bring it along. It’s like I’m never ready for that conversation to come. I need some guidance—anything to make me feel a bit better about this. Can you help? Please lead the way. Tell me, what’s really making me feel this way? I’m scared you’re going to go away and not even acknowledge me. That’s why I’m looking for ways to keep myself visible, but I’m just not sure what to say. It’s like nothing I say will cut it, and I’m afraid it’ll all come back full force. So, I just stay behind and try to push through, even if it’s only temporary success. It feels pointless, but I’m scared to let go of this idea entirely. I appreciate you stepping forward though—I promise I’ll be more open next time. Thanks for playing along. It means a lot to me that you get through that

Yeah, today was really tough. When they gave me that new project, I just felt this wave of panic hit me. I already had a bunch of things going but then all of a sudden it seemed like they put me on display there. I didn’t want to talk to anyone because I thought they’d just see how messed up I am. Playing it off sometimes made me even more scared, so every time something doesn’t go well, I just... shut down and try to ignore it. It’s like no matter what I do, it feels like it’s overwhelming me. I guess I’m afraid if I try to figure things out, I’ll just prove to myself that I’m useless. It’s frustrating because I know I should be able to handle this. But I don’t think I can. I’m sorry I can’t tell you how many times I feel that way. It’s like it’s part of me, but I just don’t want to hear it. I want to hide behind my facade of strength. It’s frustrating because I’m sure everyone else seems to be able to handle big projects. But for me, it just feels like I’m failing over and over again. I’m curious what it feels like to experience XXX’s level of anxiety and frustration right now. In the back of my mind, I wonder if maybe I’m just not cut out to tackle such massive challenges. It’s like I’m always one step behind in life, no matter how hard I try. I hope someone is watching my back, but I’m not sure I can trust them enough. So, I guess I’m just glad no one was actually there when I felt that wave of panic. It’s frustrating because I wanted to connect with them immediately. But I just kept pushing back and resist their requests, hoping maybe I could convince them of my worth. It’s like I’m stuck in a

Yeah, the meeting last week was really tough for me. I kept feeling like everyone was looking at me, like I was just another thing they didn’t want to talk to or include. I tried to keep to myself most of the time because I was so anxious and felt like my presence just proved they didn’t like me. I ended up leaving early because it was all too much for me. Honestly, I just didn’t want to deal with anything further, so I pulled out the other day. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m just not going to get around to fixing things. So, I guess I’m telling them I’m not good enough. But that thought only makes me feel even worse. Just being there doesn’t seem to help much. I’m scared I’ll break down and say something stupid or embarrassing myself. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I want change, but it never comes. My brain just won’t let go of these feelings. I’d prefer to feel like I mattered and was wanted. But I’m just not going to be able to bring myself to saying those things. It’s frustrating! I appreciate you for letting me experience this, but I’m sorry I can’t bring myself to tell you. It’s important to me that I’m loved and valued, even if just once. I hope someone can see through my doubts and realize that I’m actually worth being appreciated. Can you imagine if I just made a little bit of a difference? It feels like such a small thing could make a big difference. I love being around people, but sometimes I just wish I had that extra piece. Thank you so much for giving me a second chance. I love that you care and support me, but it still feels like a giant obstacle in my path. I appreciate you for considering it. I’ll be trying harder next time. Love,

Ugh, that thing I said about him all weekend… I just froze. I tried to explain later, but I was so overwhelmed I forgot what happened. Now I’m stuck thinking he’s being disrespectful because I’m always extra productive when we’re together. I guess I just feel like I’m making things worse instead of improving anything. It’s hard because I want to fix things, but I’m scared to face anyone. I’d rather just embarrass myself by playing pushups than have to deal with this kind of emotional pain. I don’t know—maybe I’m just not cut out to be loved properly after all. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I try harder, then I fail. But then again, I’ve never really figured out how to stop worrying about what other people think. It’s frustrating, but I can’t escape it. Thanks for playing along — I appreciate you. I really need some relief here. Please let me know if you think I got too much info. I usually just go straight to the answer. But anyway, what do you think? What did you think about XXX’s situation? I’ll say what I think, but honestly, I’m not sure what I’m talking about. It’s like I’m never going to get out of that loop. I love feeling good about myself, but right now, I just feel completely out of place. Thank you so much for coming to talk to me too! I really appreciated it. I appreciate you remembering me though. I’m glad you didn’t remember exactly what I said about him. I’ll try not to freak out about it. Can you tell me more about what happened? I’m sorry I forgot to bring it up, but I didn’t mean to start any further problems. Just needed to keep things under control. It’s hard, because I want to help, but I’m afraid to

Ugh, I’ve been thinking so much about that new project all week, and I just... froze. I spent all night working on it, double-checking everything, but it felt like I was making a perfect copy each time I tried to present. I kept telling myself it wouldn’t matter if I messed up, but then I realized I’m already pretty bad at stuff like this. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I want to start, but I’m too anxious to move forward. Honestly, it just makes me feel even worse about myself. I don’t know, it’s hard to stop feeling like I’m falling further behind. I hope someone can see how much I’m just not cut out for this. Can you help me step things up? I’d really appreciate it.

Um, well… last night was kind of rough, I guess. When the partner said they wanted to go to this fancy restaurant, I just stayed quiet and agreed because I didn’t want to seem disagreeable or maybe cause a fight. I noticed how I was always trying to check if they were liking it or not, even though they weren’t really expressing it. It’s like, why not keep avoiding the real thing? I feel kind of invisible or out of place, like I’m just there waiting for something to go wrong. It’s hard to stop myself from thinking that maybe I’m actually just not liked enough. I don’t know what to say next without being judged or rejected. Just like that in that moment with them. It’s exhausting, but I can’t get past it. So, I ended up hiding the menu and made sure nothing looked too good or wasn’t telling anyone how I felt. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how to change things. What do you think? I need some real answers right away. Or maybe you’ve got a better idea for how to deal with this than what I just said. I’m sorry I came off so negative, but I just felt so out of place. It makes me want to leave quickly. But I’m stuck here, so I feel trapped. Can you give me some guidance on how to talk through this without just going along? I’d really appreciate it! 😂 🙅 ️⃝

I will tell you about the gathering later, when I just couldn’t bring myself to say anything. It was like trying to jump through a fire — no matter what I tried, I just ended up leaving early. I feel like I’m always falling short in those situations, like nothing I do touches their eyes or ears. It’s frustrating because I want to speak up, but it feels like I’m making things worse. I guess I just don’t know how to hold my own in this family dynamic. I prefer not to be involved altogether because I worry it won’t solve anything. Just knowing someone else will be disappointed doesn’t give me any reason to feel proud anymore. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I try to improve, but it always ends up feeling worthless. I want to be seen as important, but instead, I feel so insignificant. What’s next? How am I supposed to fix this? I love being around people, but right now, it feels like I’m just there, not part of something. Thank you so much for playing! I appreciate you laughing with me. I’ll be surprised again. It’s like I’m always stuck in this cycle where I don’t belong. I’d really like some guidance or a different path forward. Please let me know what to expect. I love being challenged, but I also hate feeling like I’m just an extra in a whole bigger game. I need something stronger than that family thing to work. Helpless? That’s how I see it—like I’m always letting everyone down. It’s exhausting, but I can’t shake the feeling that I’m stuck in this unwinnable game. I’m sorry I came across that, I just felt like saying something would just make everything worse. I want to be better, but I’m scared it won’t even come close. Can you think of a better way to

Uh, well, last week when they mentioned separating, I immediately went into some crazy thinking about changing plans. I said things like “Why don’t we just go somewhere different” and then offered all sorts of ideas for what I’d like to do afterward. But then, after I made those changes, they started asking about things they already wanted to do, like being around more of that family I already know. It felt like they were readying themselves to say I didn’t feel right there. I just kept suppressing my doubts because I didn’t want them getting hurt or leaving me. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where staying just doesn’t work out for me. I guess I’m scared that if I try too hard to keep up, they’ll turn against me once they’re gone. It’s exhausting being worried about how much I’m doing for them versus not feeling really loved or respected. I want to stop, but I’m not sure I can just say no without making it sound easier than this. So, I just keep pushing through, even though it’s hurting me deeply. It’s like I’m afraid to talk about these feelings, so I stay quiet and just accept what’s happening. I wish I could change things, but I’m just stuck in this loop where I’m convinced I’m destined to be left. Just remember, though, sometimes the hardest things happen first. Thanks so much for asking! I love being the patient you love. But honestly, I’m just plain uncomfortable with how I’m feeling right now. I’m not sure what to say instead of just saying what’s going on. It’s like I’m never going to escape this loop. What do you think? Or how should I start talking? Or do you have any idea what to say next? I’d love to get past this, but I’m so stuck. Please let me tell you what

Yeah, this situation really hits home how overwhelmed and stuck I am at work. I find myself just standing there, double-checking everything over and over, hoping it’s okay I’m done feeling so bad about myself after all those mistakes. It’s like I’m scared that if I mess up even a little, everyone will see me as a complete failure. I know I should be pushing ahead, but I keep avoiding groups because the thought of seeing someone judge me makes me freeze up. It’s hard to break free and concentrate on my work when I’m completely lost. I just want to keep away from everyone else so I don’t look like a failure. I guess I keep reminding myself that I’m not a good person, and that’s just not true. I love where I’m at right now, but I’m scared to go forward. It’s like I’m always waiting for someone to say something stupid or dismiss me altogether. I’d much rather not face those kinds of situations head-on. What do you think?

I won’t even let myself go to the dinner. It felt like such a waste—like I’m just sitting there feeling invisible, like nobody cares how much I’ve succeeded or failed. I know I’m making mistakes, but it’s hard to stop feeling like I’m letting everyone down too. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s always going to be wrong or disappointing. I want to connect, but my mind just stops, and I feel so stuck in my own little world. It’s frustrating and overwhelming all at once. I don’t know why I’m even trying to change anything. Everyone else seems to be thriving, and here I am, feeling like a failure. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I’m scared of being seen as a disappointment. What’s the point of trying when it always ends badly? I’ll probably just leave early and go back to sleep. It’s easy to fall into that pattern where I just stay away instead. Can you believe, though, I feel like this must be part of something bigger than I can understand? I’m sorry I can’t do what I want or feel more connected. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I can’t move forward or feel free to grow. What’s stopping me from giving up and starting again? It’s frustrating, and I feel like I’m failing to make things right. I guess I just don’t trust myself to see through the comparisons. I’m afraid to prove myself, and then people will think less of me. I wish I could just shake off and move on, you know, maybe feel some hope that maybe I’m just not meant to be this person. But no, it’s not going the way I wanted it to. Please, therapist, just let me know what’s really happening, and I’d be more comfortable. I need someone to see through the facade and confirm

Yeah, I guess I just didn’t feel ready to go. I kept thinking, “Okay, maybe they’re right, I’m just too much trouble for them to stay.” It’s like when I try to join in, things just fall apart because I don’t come through for everyone. I keep wondering why they’d want me there if they could all just leave me out instead of liking me anyway. I’ve been sitting alone late at night thinking about those times I didn’t end up being accepted, and it just makes me feel worse, doesn’t help. It’s hard not to feel invisible sometimes, even when I’m surrounded by people. I hope I can just forget about it and head off to enjoy myself, but it just feels impossible without pushing myself further. Why not let go and try something different? That would seem simpler and less daunting. But then again, I’m used to fearing acceptance forever. So, what do you think?

Yeah, the dinner was rough. When my sibling got recognized, I just felt like I wasn’t even there because I’m invisible. I ended up just sitting quietly because I didn’t want to cause any tension or seem needy. It’s like everyone around me is already winning out compared to me, no matter what I accomplish. I keep thinking maybe I’m just as flawed as I feel. Honestly, it’s hard to stop that internal voice from telling me I’ve let people down again.
